new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a wine of fresh grapes certifying that it carries a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) as defined in EU wine regulation, distinguishing it from wines with a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) and wines without any geographical indication.

new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) in containers ≤ 2 L
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter geografischer Angabe (g.g.A.) in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2204 21 79/80)
new_classes.definition_en: A wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) quality, presented in packaging whose volume does not exceed 2 litres. Covers CN codes 2204 21 79 and 2204 21 80, discriminated from PDO wines in small containers (2204 21 1x–2x) and non-geographical-indication wines in small containers (2204 21 84/87/88).

coverage_explanation: The two core distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 21 80 are captured: (1) the wine bears a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), modelled via the new PGIOriginQuality class and a has-quality restriction; (2) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 L, modelled via the existing SmallContainer class and a has-part restriction. Coverage is reduced to 0.75 because: (a) the legal note cross-references 'additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22', which defines specific PGI alcohol-strength and total-dry-extract sub-ranges that further discriminate CN 2204 21 79 from 2204 21 80 — these numerical thresholds (alcohol by volume and/or total dry extract ranges) are not expressible from the information provided in the legal note text alone and would require the full text of additional note 6(a); (b) the sibling split between 22042179 and 22042180 (both are PGI wines in ≤ 2 L containers) presumably rests on an actual alcoholic strength or total dry extract criterion from that additional note, which cannot be axiomatised without access to note 6(a)'s numerical thresholds. The PGIOriginQuality complement restriction over PDOOriginQuality is expressed as a complement axiom to enforce disjointness between PGI and PDO status.
